title: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR EDIBLE SAGO FLOUR Asia CXS 301R-2011 Adopted in 2011. Amended in 2023.

sections.section: REGIONAL STANDARD FOR EDIBLE SAGO FLOUR Asia CXS 301R-2011 Adopted in 2011. Amended in 2023.
sections.summary: Title and identification of the regional standard: 'Regional Standard for Edible Sago Flour (Asia) CXS 301R-2011', adopted 2011 and amended 2023.
Notice that amendments adopted by the Codex Alimentarius Commission in December 2023 revised Section 7.2 on labelling of non-retail containers.
Scope: standard applies to edible sago flour from the pith/soft core of Metroxylon spp. intended for direct human consumption and explicitly excludes products obtained from cassava tubers (tapioca).

Describes the product: edible sago flour is produced from the pith or soft core of palm trees (Metroxylon spp.) by mechanical treatment followed by soaking, settling and drying (see product definition).
Defines edible sago flour as the product prepared from the pith or soft core of Metroxylon spp. by mechanical treatment (pounding, grinding, milling) followed by soaking, settling and drying.

Section heading covering essential composition and quality factors, including general requirements (free from off‑flavours, odours and filth) and specific compositional and quality limits detailed in 3.2.
Requires edible sago flour to be free from off‑flavours and odours and to be free from filth (impurities of animal origin, including dead insects) and other extraneous matter.

Specifies numeric quality limits for edible sago flour: moisture ≤13% m/m; ash (inorganic extraneous matter) ≤0.5% m/m; acidity ≤220 mg KOH/100 g; starch ≥65% m/m; crude fibre ≤0.1% m/m; particle size such that ≥95% passes a 100‑mesh sieve; colour described as from white to light‑brown.
Permits flour treatment agents listed in Tables 1 and 2 of the General Standard for Food Additives (CXS 192‑1995) for use in foods conforming to this standard under food category 06.2.1 'Flours'.
Requires products to comply with the General Standard for Contaminants and Toxins in Food and Feed (CXS 193‑1995) and with Codex maximum residue limits for pesticides.

Recommends preparation and handling in accordance with the General Principles of Food Hygiene (CXC 1‑1969) and other relevant Codex codes, and requires compliance with any microbiological criteria established per CXG 21‑1997.

States that labelling must follow the General Standard for the Labelling of Pre‑packaged Foods (CXS 1‑1985) and adds specific provisions including the product name (7.1) and non‑retail container labelling (7.2).
Requires the product name on the label to be 'Edible sago flour'.
Requires labelling of non‑retail containers to be in accordance with the General Standard for the Labelling of Non‑Retail Containers of Foods (CXS 346‑2021).

Packaging requirements: edible sago flour shall be packed in containers that safeguard its hygienic, nutritional, technological and organoleptic qualities, and containers/materials must be safe, suitable and not impart toxic substances or undesirable odour or flavour.
Specifies methods of analysis and sampling: moisture by ISO 712:1998; ash by ISO 2171:2007; acidity by AOAC 939.05; crude fibre by ISO 6541:1981; and starch by AOAC 920.44.
